segments: Sickle Cell Disease and Transfusion-Dependent Beta Thalassemia

dependencies: Third-party contract manufacturers for majority of commercial supply
Single sourced critical steps and key materials for manufacturing process
Global supply chain for raw materials sourcing
Third-party manufacturers for CASGEVY cell culture reagents and gene-editing components
Complex manufacturing for biological and cell/genetic therapies requiring different systems, equipment, facilities, and expertise
risks: Supply chain disruptions from geopolitical actions including war and terrorism
Production shortages from events affecting raw material supply or manufacturing capabilities abroad
Import and export licensing requirements, tariffs, and trade restrictions
Reliance on third-party manufacturers including some in China
Potential inability of third-party manufacturers to meet requirements
Manufacturing facilities subject to FDA and foreign government inspections
Regulatory approval delays for manufacturing facilities
Single sourced critical manufacturing steps and materials
Coordination challenges for CASGEVY manufacturing across multiple facilities
Potential U.S. legislation limiting use of Chinese biotechnology equipment/services
Risk of Chinese service providers being restricted from U.S. business

regulatory_risks: EMA and foreign regulatory authority approval processes
Post-approval regulatory oversight and potential restrictions
Clinical trial regulatory requirements and potential holds
Cell and gene therapy specific regulatory scrutiny
Potential revised regulatory requirements for cell/genetic therapies
Healthcare fraud and abuse laws (Anti-Kickback Statute, False Claims Act)
Inflation Reduction Act drug pricing provisions
Data privacy regulations (GDPR, state privacy laws)
Environmental, health and workplace safety regulations
Potential compulsory licensing or march-in rights
BIOSECURE Act potential restrictions on Chinese suppliers

sanction_risks: Changes in tariffs, trade barriers, and regulatory requirements
Potential restrictions on Chinese biotechnology service providers
Legislation limiting use of Chinese equipment/services
Sanctions affecting ability to conduct business with specific vendors
Trade restrictions impacting foreign third-party manufacturers and suppliers
